# Research cold-email drafts for Celina Lin

Review the employer posting and lab website before sending. Replace generic recipients with a specific PI or hiring manager when possible.

## lululemon — Product Operations Educator / Garden State Plaza

**Subject:** Interest in Product Operations Educator / Garden State Plaza — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Product Operations Educator / Garden State Plaza opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5443a9e726ec56126a9a1b

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. Dear Greenhouse Software Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Before/After School Preschool Teacher - Today's Kids opportunity with Greenhouse Software at Greenhouse Software.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a63140899515267a6f012ab

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Part-Time Educator / Hyde Park Village opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Research Coordinator A/B (OBGYN - Pregnancy & Perinatal Research Center) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear University of Pennsylvania Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Coordinator A/B (OBGYN - Pregnancy & Perinatal Research Center) opportunity with University of Pennsylvania at University of Pennsylvania.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a54f8b0268af95237be866d

**Subject:** Interest in Post Doc - Open Rank - Wolfe Lab — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear UMass Chan Medical School Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Post Doc - Open Rank - Wolfe Lab opportunity with UMass Chan Medical School at UMass Chan Medical School.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a61dc70ab14335fc0f1a0eb

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5aeb823ac7627fe9006645

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Laboratory Technician - Gas Chromatography opportunity with SGS at SGS.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

I am reaching out about the Migrant Education Recruiter - Student Support Specialist - Parent Involvement Specialist - Washington opportunity with Northwest Tri-County Intermediate Unit 5 (IU5) at Northwest Tri-County Intermediate Unit 5 (IU5).

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Assistant Program Coordinator-South Shore opportunity with May Institute at May Institute.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

**Subject:** Interest in 3D Printing Instructor, School Break Camps — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear JCC Greater Boston Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the 3D Printing Instructor, School Break Camps opportunity with JCC Greater Boston at JCC Greater Boston.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a4f0f4915b4965afd5854ec

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the SolarCorps Workforce Development Fellow (Stipend) opportunity with GRID Alternatives at GRID Alternatives.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,

## Children's Hospital of Philadelphia — Clinical Research Assistant I - Neurology

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Research Assistant I - Neurology — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Children's Hospital of Philadelphia Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Assistant I - Neurology opportunity with Children's Hospital of Philadelphia at Children's Hospital of Philadelphia.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Boston Children's Hospital — Research Assistant I

**Subject:** Interest in Research Assistant I — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Boston Children's Hospital Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research Assistant I opportunity with Boston Children's Hospital at Boston Children's Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Rocket Lab — Software Engineer I/II

**Subject:** Interest in Software Engineer I/II — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Rocket Lab Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Software Engineer I/II opportunity with Rocket Lab at Rocket Lab.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Dallas College — Administrative Support-Student Assistant (Basic Needs RedBird Workforce Center))

**Subject:** Interest in Administrative Support-Student Assistant (Basic Needs RedBird Workforce Center)) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Dallas College Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Administrative Support-Student Assistant (Basic Needs RedBird Workforce Center)) opportunity with Dallas College at Dallas College.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Unit Secretary - Emergency Department — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Centinela Hospital Medical Center Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Unit Secretary - Emergency Department opportunity with Centinela Hospital Medical Center at Centinela Hospital Medical Center.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Cincinnati — Workforce Development Program Leader - Buenger Club

**Subject:** Interest in Workforce Development Program Leader - Buenger Club — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Cincinnati Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Workforce Development Program Leader - Buenger Club opportunity with Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Cincinnati at Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Cincinnati.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Youth Development Counselor- Stephens opportunity with YMCA of Greater Houston at YMCA of Greater Houston.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the 2026 Spring Business Operation Specialist + Math Content Teacher opportunity with Think Academy U.S at Think Academy U.S.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. **Subject:** Interest in Clinical Research Assistant - Oncology — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Scripps Health Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Assistant - Oncology opportunity with Scripps Health at Scripps Health.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Paraprofessional II (SPED) (PT)- 288 opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5fbcbb193b51130967a62f

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Teacher - High- Math 6-12 opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Evergreen Paraeducator 1:1 Student Support opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a61ef0d11edf44d79163378

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. **Subject:** Interest in Bilingual (Fluent in Creole) Support Teacher - 2026/2027 School Year — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Mastery Schools Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Bilingual (Fluent in Creole) Support Teacher - 2026/2027 School Year opportunity with Mastery Schools at Mastery Schools.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a4b2a4e4eb370649b2799ff

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the School Secretary opportunity with Academy School District 20 at Academy School District 20.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5f944cb0f20036bc631a44

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Youth Development Counselor - Campbell Elementary (Conroe) opportunity with YMCA of Greater Houston at YMCA of Greater Houston.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## SchoolSpring — Paraprofessional, English Learner Program (Part-Time) - Temporary (Starts at $17.50 per hour)

**Subject:** Interest in Paraprofessional, English Learner Program (Part-Time) - Temporary (Starts at $17.50 per hour) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Paraprofessional, English Learner Program (Part-Time) - Temporary (Starts at $17.50 per hour) opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Teacher - ESE- Varying Exceptionalities opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5e32c227bf767ea68f5394

---

## Murphy Research — Research Associate

**Subject:** Interest in Research Associate — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Murphy Research Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research Associate opportunity with Murphy Research at Murphy Research.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## East Side Union High School District — CSEA Transfer Opportunity - Special Education Paraeducator 2 - Walters (Internal only PC: 1740)

**Subject:** Interest in CSEA Transfer Opportunity - Special Education Paraeducator 2 - Walters (Internal only PC: 1740) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear East Side Union High School District Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the CSEA Transfer Opportunity - Special Education Paraeducator 2 - Walters (Internal only PC: 1740) opportunity with East Side Union High School District at East Side Union High School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am reaching out about the Healthcare Customer Service & Administrative Careers Training Program opportunity with Boston Children's Hospital at Boston Children's Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Program / Project Coordinator I opportunity with University of Missouri-Columbia at University of Missouri-Columbia.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Mastery Teaching Residency - Philadelphia, PA - SY 2026-27 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Mastery Schools Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Mastery Teaching Residency - Philadelphia, PA - SY 2026-27 opportunity with Mastery Schools at Mastery Schools.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Middlebury Youth Development Professional Lead opportunity with Boys & Girls Clubs of Elkhart County at Boys & Girls Clubs of Elkhart County.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5bcaa23ac7627fe90091c9

## Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology — Career Mentor / Data Assistant

**Subject:** Interest in Career Mentor / Data Assistant — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Career Mentor / Data Assistant opportunity with Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology at Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## SchoolSpring — Instructional Assistant, Pre Kinder

**Subject:** Interest in Instructional Assistant, Pre Kinder — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Instructional Assistant, Pre Kinder opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Life Science Research Professional 1 - SCI (OnSite) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Stanford University Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Life Science Research Professional 1 - SCI (OnSite) opportunity with Stanford University at Stanford University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Training Specialist opportunity with Werfen at Werfen.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Soar Autism Center — Behavior Analyst in Training - Paradise Valley

**Subject:** Interest in Behavior Analyst in Training - Paradise Valley — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Soar Autism Center Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Behavior Analyst in Training - Paradise Valley opportunity with Soar Autism Center at Soar Autism Center.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Dear Boys & Girls Clubs of South Central Kansas Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Child Care: Youth Development Professional (YDP) for After School Program in Maize ($14 per hour) opportunity with Boys & Girls Clubs of South Central Kansas at Boys & Girls Clubs of South Central Kansas.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a29b15ec07d4b6ae1c4245a

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research Data Engineer 1 opportunity with University of Rochester at University of Rochester.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I am especially interested in the lab's use of single-cell and spatial transcriptomic data to connect molecular mechanisms with therapeutic questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built reproducible genomics workflows and enjoy translating complex biological questions into clear, testable computational analyses.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/modal/7db771eb-e379-47fe-974f-765a8780dc10

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Associate, Cyber Resilience — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Pfizer Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate, Cyber Resilience opportunity with Pfizer at Pfizer.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://pfizer.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/PfizerCareers/job/Greece-Thessaloniki-Chortiatis/Associate--Cyber-Resilience_4961021

**Subject:** Interest in Associate Clinical Oncology Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Clinical Oncology Specialist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources.
